compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can validate cross-cutting ticket scenarios through E2E tests
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: E2E test exercising cross-cutting ticket scenarios where compact-on-close affects downstream operations
**Why**: The compact-on-close feature deletes original event files (including CREATE events), breaking ghost checks in comment/edit/transition/revert scripts — this was a real CI failure pattern
**Scope**:
- IN: Create ticket, close (triggers compact-on-close), then comment/edit/transition on compacted ticket
- OUT: Non-compact ticket scenarios (covered by Story 2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, an E2E test creates a ticket, transitions to closed (triggering compact-on-close), then successfully runs comment, edit, and transition on the compacted ticket without errors
  ← Satisfies: "E2E tests exercise cross-cutting scenarios: compact-on-close followed by comment/edit/transition"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] This test directly validates the ghost check fix (c40b-42e6) — if ghost check logic changes, this test must be updated
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
